meaning: n.留下阴影,描影法,明暗法,底纹,遮蔽
examples: The conservatory will get very hot in summer unless shading is used.
Use the same tool to draw in hair, neck shading, etc.
Define a new table style with shading of the first row.
The Public Garden is most famous for its weeping willows shading the central lagoon.
Black shading adds depth and allows the artist to fit the design within the body's contours.
